evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Auth0 React SDK usage (Auth0Provider, loginWithRedirect, getAccessTokenSilently) is internally consistent with current SDK shape. However, backend uses jwt.get_unverified_claims (no signature verification) for the protected endpoint — functionally incorrect for RBAC enforcement though syntactically valid. auth0-fastapi package symbols (Auth0Config, AuthClient, register_auth_routes) cannot be verified; long-tail handling applies — consistent internally with claimed version.
evidence.executability: App.jsx references useAuth0 without import (undefined symbol) — runtime ReferenceError. main.py imports succeed only if auth0-fastapi exposes those exact symbols (unverified). git clone of external repos is sandbox-feasible but mixing cloned-repo code with inline main.py creates ambiguity for the extractor about which file wins. Test file imports application.main which depends on env vars at import time — may fail. Multiple non-obvious fixes needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) acquisition steps present and detailed; (b) bash install blocks present; (c) env template present with exact names; (d) backend code in fenced block; (e) frontend code present; (f) test command present. Env vars listed match those referenced. Some duplicate setup content (clone + inline code) creates conflicting samples. Light on which file path inline code overwrites in the cloned repo.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Official auth0.com/docs and developer.auth0.com URLs cited at end with inline [1]-[4] markers near relevant sections. No fabricated URLs detected. Placement is end-of-doc with inline reference numbers rather than full URLs near code.

rubric_notes: App.jsx missing useAuth0 import is a clear executability defect. Backend RBAC check uses unverified JWT claims — security flaw acknowledged later in section 8.4 but the extracted main.py is what runs.
